narrative: US Treasury revoking Iran's crude oil waiver and Strait of Hormuz tensions introduce a geopolitical risk premium, driving WTI higher and lifting the dollar as equities slip — gold lower -1.09%; equities -0.45%; DXY +0.27%; VIX 16.1. USD/JPY holds at 162.09, supported by a +266bp OIS carry advantage, while options show a strong put skew with a -1.52 25d RR (Saxo, 1M, indicative mid). NZD/USD slips to 0.5678 as the RBNZ's negative real rate of -0.83% faces a knife-edge policy decision. Watch the next RBNZ meeting.
